shelfmark: UTO Sinai Greek NF M 90, SPP No. 362

note: Written in a medium-sized hand with average competence

The folios from this object were originally independent bifolios sewn together from a much smaller Ethiopian book to form a bifolio for the new Greek book, oriented at 90 degrees to the overtext. The two bifolios were from an Ethiopian book whose size was approximately half the size of the new Greek book, thus when sewn together and trimmed, they provided a sheet of the right size and shape for the new Greek book. The seam is now in the gutter of the Greek book in its current shape. The gutter folds of the original Ethiopic sheets are still visible, oriented at 90 degrees to the orientation of the current Greek codex

There are eight occurrences in the text of rubricated headings for the hours, indicating both the hour and the text to be read (e.g., Gospel, Acts) | There are no earlier attestations of this Horologion text

cataloguer.message: Description and identification of undertexts as part of SPP (2011-2016). Migrated to SMDL in 2025.

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
